rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF AMBER ALEXANDRA CUMMINGS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/01/2022. 1) There is no such filing as the 11/7/2021 Notice of Errata. Guardian must either file an amendment, amended petition, or a supplement and such filing must be signed and verified by the Guardian. [See CRC 7.3 and 7.103]. 2) Please explain the proper bond. The petition alleges an increase in bond will be filed to $616,000.00. The 11/17/2021 Notice of Errata alleges the bond at $546,000.00 is sufficient. The 12/2/2021 Ex Parte Application alleges bond should be increased to $589,000.00. An account will not be approved until the additional bond is on file. [LRSF 14.16 and LRSF 14.11 regarding Ex Parte Petitions.] 3) Page 2 lines 13-15 alleges the first account was approved. The court order says the account was settled and allowed not approved. 4) Page 2 lines 15-17 and page 4 lines 24-25 allege the court also approved investments. The court order says the investments are accepted not approved. 5) Please explain the request for $433 for filing this petition when the filing fee was $435.00. 6) Please explain the 11/2/2020 Required minimum distributions to beneficiary entries totaling $1,929.32. 7) Please file the account statement for account ending in #4422 for June 1, 2021 to June 30, 2021. Guardian filed for 10/14/20-10/31/20 and 07/01/2021-07/31/2021. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 8) Please file invoices for all preferred Legal Services. Please explain why using Preferred legal Services was a benefit to the guardianship and not just to the attorney. 9) Please explain why the attorney is requesting $9.00 for reimbursement for parking when LRSF 14.58 says parking is absorbed as part of fees. The court will not approve requests for reimbursement of parking. 10) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARJORIE MILLER COMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The administrator retained unlawful detainer litigation counsel to recover possession of the estate real property from David Comer, one of the heirs, and paid more than $24,000 from the estate funds to the litigation counsel without prior court approval. The administrator alleges that she had authority under Probate Code section 10532(a) to pay the litigation counsel's fees from the estate without prior court approval. 2) The administrator settled the unlawful detainer litigation against David Comer and another occupant of the estate real property and paid them $27,500 from the estate funds without giving notice of proposed action. The administrator gave notice of proposed action more than a year after the settlement. 3) The decedent died in 1977, before the California inheritance tax was repealed in 1982. No inheritance tax report has been filed by the probate referee, and no order fixing the tax has been issued in this proceeding. 4) The administrator proposes deducting $28,105.10 from David Comer's 1/3 share of net cash distribution for his failure to timely vacate the estate's real property in breach of the settlement agreement. The administrator proposes further distribution of the $28,105.10 in equal shares to the 3 heirs including David Comer. 5) The administrator's attorneys request compensation in the amount of $20,340 for extraordinary services to the estate. The amount includes $742.50 charged by the attorneys' paralegal to "draft ex parte petition to retain real estate attorney, order and notice" which was never filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MABLE LEE FERNANDEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/01/2022. 1) Please explain why the Inventory and Appraisal Attachment 1 says "Estate of Mable Lee Fernandez Keith Moore ADM". The I&A must show accounts at the Decedent's date of death and it appears this is an estate account. 2) The "Declaration of Yaasha Sabba, Esa/ Correcting Typographical Error in Petition for Final Distribution" is not a proper filing. [See CRC 7.3]. 3) The request/prayer in the petition page 10 does not state who the estate is to be distributed to. Page 10 paragraph 6 says "as set forth in Paragraphs 36 and 38 above. Paragraph 36 appears to be regarding preliminary distributions and paragraph 38 appears to be regarding bond. [See LRSF 14.36 I. 6) a.] 4) The $6,606.45 number on page 5 lines 20 and 22 does not appear to be correct. Petition alleges the total fee is $7551.45 - $1,155 = $6396.45. 25% of $6396.45 = $1599.11. $6395.45 - $1599.11 = $4.797.34. 5) Please file a copy of the money judgment against Terrence Chilton entered on July 17, 2020 in the amount of $10,789.32. [See LRSF 14.36 B.] 6) Note: petition page 7 line 14 there is no LRSF 14.67 E. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
